checks.finding: T-001 through T-005 change fail-closed review, provenance, lifecycle, repository, CI, or quality-gate integrity boundaries and justify Risk: high; T-002 also performs bounded classification migration. T-006 is reversible release metadata with observable behavior and is appropriately medium.

In tasks.md, T-001 through T-005 Done When require only an unspecified 'Independent review verdict'; none names a second reviewer or requires that reviewer to differ from the implementing agent. T-002 through T-005 also require a Red→Green log without explicitly requiring the failing test to be captured before implementation begins. These omissions leave mandatory high-risk independence and TDD sequencing unverifiable; the check contract classifies missing high-risk evidence as Critical.
T-001 through T-006 each define one coherent deliverable, contain five to seven Done When items, and separate registry, validator, integration, gate, and release-metadata work through blockers.

Error-path evidence is planned: T-001 covers traversal, escape, stale hashes, and forgery; T-002 maps AC-002/AC-006; T-003 maps AC-004/AC-005/AC-014; T-004 maps invalid-state AC-009; T-005 requires integration/regression assertions preserving predecessor checks; T-006 requires a pre-bump version-consistency failure.
SKIP: no standalone task declares itself as a unit, integration, end-to-end, or acceptance test task.
Every high-risk task T-001 through T-005 has an explicit Rollback field and a Done When item requiring the rollback procedure to be documented or exercised.

Shared files are coordinated by dependency order and distinct purpose: T-001 precedes T-005 transitively through T-003 for precheck scripts, and T-004 precedes T-006 for tests/validate-repository.ps1. No uncoordinated same-purpose overlap is present.
T-003 genuinely consumes T-001 transition repair and T-002 registry; T-004 and T-005 consume T-003 validation; T-006 follows both integration paths before synchronized release metadata is finalized.
T-001 explicitly captures the Spec-Passed to Impl-precheck failure, diagnoses the canonical hash/path mismatch before implementation, and requires the reproduction plus Red→Green regression evidence.
